common.siteDescription: Open-source platform for Madagascar's endemic freshwater fish — species profiles, distribution, and conservation coordination.

hero.title: A shared record for Madagascar's endemic freshwater fish.
hero.subtitle: An open platform that brings together species profiles, ex-situ breeding coordination, and field program tracking for the roughly 79 freshwater fish species found only in Madagascar. Built to complement IUCN, GBIF, FishBase, and ZIMS — not replace them.

keeping.noteCares: A CARES priority species — hobbyist breeders play a direct conservation role.

iucnDescriptions.CR: Faces an extremely high risk of extinction in the wild. Populations have collapsed or occupy a very narrow range, and urgent intervention is required.
iucnDescriptions.EN: Faces a very high risk of extinction in the wild. Populations are fragmented or declining steeply enough to threaten persistence.
iucnDescriptions.VU: Faces a high risk of extinction in the wild. Threats are significant enough that decline is likely without conservation action.
iucnDescriptions.NT: Close to qualifying for a threatened category, or likely to qualify in the near future without continued monitoring.
iucnDescriptions.LC: Widespread and abundant. Does not currently qualify for a threatened category.
iucnDescriptions.DD: Inadequate information is available to make a direct or indirect assessment of extinction risk.
iucnDescriptions.NE: Has not yet been evaluated against IUCN Red List criteria.

captive.title: Captive population summary
captive.noneTracked: No captive population is currently tracked.
captive.noneTrackedThreatened: This species is threatened and has no ex-situ safety net. Contact the registry to register a holding.
captive.noneTrackedNonThreatened: No institution has registered holdings for this species.

field.title: In-situ linkages

sentence.default: Guidance on keeping this species, drawn from keepers and published sources.

sourcingEthics.heading: Sourcing ethics
sourcingEthics.para1: Keeping Malagasy freshwater fish in captivity is part of how these species survive. Habitat loss, introduced predators, and altered hydrology have reduced wild populations to the point that some species now depend on coordinated ex-situ (captive) programs for a demographic safety net. That work requires wild-sourced founders and, occasionally, genetic refreshment — it cannot be done from captive stock alone indefinitely.
sourcingEthics.para2: Responsible wild collection happens under permit, by programs working in partnership with Malagasy authorities and in-country researchers, usually tied to a specific institutional or CARES breeding plan. It is documented, reported, and accountable. Rescue collections after habitat-loss events — dam construction, wetland drainage, pollution incidents — have also been a source of founder stock for some of the most imperiled species. This is conservation work, not the aquarium trade.
sourcingEthics.para3: Hobbyists should not collect from the wild on trips or through informal exporters. Instead, source fish from CARES-registered breeders, Citizen Conservation, or partner institutions; document lineage where known; and share surplus fry back into the breeder network so founder genetics are preserved across multiple keepers. A well-distributed captive population is worth more to the species than any single tank of wild-caught fish.
sourcingEthics.footerLine: More on how the platform handles data, sources, and sensitive locations: <dataLink>how we handle the data</dataLink>.

page.metaTitle: About — Madagascar Freshwater Fish Conservation Platform
page.metaDescription: About the Madagascar Freshwater Fish Conservation Platform: mission, data sources, ownership, and citations.
page.heroTitle: A shared record of Madagascar's endemic freshwater fish.
page.heroSubtitle: An open platform for species profiles, ex-situ breeding coordination, and field program tracking — built to complement IUCN, GBIF, FishBase, and ZIMS, not replace them.
page.missionPara1: Madagascar's freshwater fish are the most imperiled vertebrate group on the island. Of the {speciesTotal} described and undescribed endemic species in the registry, the majority are assessed as threatened on the IUCN Red List, and a significant share have no known captive population to serve as a demographic safety net. Coordination across the institutions working on these species — zoos, aquariums, academic researchers, hobbyist breeders, and in-country field programs — has historically relied on email threads, personal networks, and one-off spreadsheets.
page.missionPara2: This platform is a single, open record of what is known: which species exist, how they are assessed, where they have been observed, and which institutions hold captive populations. It aggregates from authoritative upstream sources rather than competing with them, and publishes back to them (via Darwin Core Archives) where the data flow is appropriate.
page.scopePara1: Public profiles cover every endemic species in the registry, including undescribed morphospecies that do not yet appear on the IUCN Red List. Conservation status mirrors the most recent accepted assessment; species with no assessment are marked "not yet assessed" rather than given a stale category. The distribution map shows locality records generalized per GBIF sensitive-species protocols; exact coordinates for threatened species are restricted to coordinator-tier accounts to protect wild populations.
page.scopePara2: Restricted tiers support ex-situ breeding coordination, transfer recommendations, and studbook-level data for registered partners. Those features are not visible on the public site.
page.provenancePara1: Species and assessment data are pulled from the IUCN Red List and FishBase. Occurrence records follow the Darwin Core standard and can be published to GBIF. Captive-population figures are entered by partner institutions and, where available, reconciled with ZIMS (Species360). Priority lists from SHOAL (1,000 Fishes Blueprint) and CARES inform filtering and sort order but do not override IUCN categories.
page.dwcLine: The platform's Darwin Core Archive — used by GBIF's Integrated Publishing Toolkit — is available for direct download as <dwcLink>mffcp-occurrences.zip</dwcLink>. Threatened-species coordinates are generalized to a 0.1° (≈11 km) grid; exact coordinates remain on the platform behind the coordinator-tier gate.
page.provenancePara2: For a fuller account of provenance, the mirror policy for IUCN status, coordinate generalization, and known limitations, see <dataLink>how we handle the data</dataLink>.
page.provenancePara3: Definitions of IUCN categories, CARES, Darwin Core, and other terminology used across the site are collected in the <glossaryLink>glossary</glossaryLink>.
page.stewardshipBody: Maintained by Aleksei Saunders (Wildlife Protection Solutions). Long-term stewardship is under discussion with partner organizations. Source code: <repoLink>github.com/alexsaunderswps/madagascarfish</repoLink>. Licensed Apache-2.0.
page.citation1Prefix: Leiss L, Rauhaus A, Rakotoarison A, Fusari C, Vences M, Ziegler T. Review of threatened Malagasy freshwater fishes in zoos and aquaria: The necessity of an ex situ conservation network — A call for action.
page.citation2: IUCN. Red List of Threatened Species. Version consulted at build time; category and criteria mirrored from the most recent accepted assessment per species.
page.citation3: SHOAL. 1,000 Fishes Blueprint — global conservation priorities for freshwater fishes.
page.citation4: GBIF. Darwin Core Archive standard and sensitive-species coordinate-generalization guidance.

data.metaTitle: How we handle the data — Madagascar Freshwater Fish Conservation Platform
data.metaDescription: Where the platform's species data comes from, how conservation status is mirrored from IUCN, how sensitive locations are generalized, and the limitations we are honest about.
data.title: How we handle the data
data.intro: The platform aggregates from authoritative upstream sources and tries to be explicit about provenance, limitations, and what is deliberately not public. This page explains how.

sources.title: Where species data comes from
sources.lead: The registry combines records from several upstream sources, each authoritative for a different slice of the picture:
sources.iucnBody: — the source of record for conservation assessments (category, criteria, assessment date, assessors).
sources.fishbaseBody: — ecological and morphological data (habitat, maximum length, diet notes).
sources.gbifBody: — occurrence records (georeferenced observations and museum specimens) published under Darwin Core.
sources.caresBody: — the Conservation, Awareness, Recognition and Encouragement for Species priority list maintained by the fishkeeping-hobbyist community, distinct from the IUCN Red List and focused on species held in private breeding programs.
sources.shoalBody: — global priority alignment for freshwater fish conservation.
sources.expertBody: — named researchers and keepers who have published or shared records for Malagasy endemics. Individual contributors are credited on the records they informed.

policy.title: Conservation status is mirrored, not edited
policy.para1: The IUCN category shown on every species profile is a mirror of the most recent accepted assessment for that species. The platform does not hand-edit the badge. When a new IUCN assessment is ingested, the mirror updates; when no assessment exists, the profile reads "not yet assessed" rather than showing a stale category.
policy.para2: Manual expert input is supported, but only by creating a new assessment record with an assessor, date, and reasoning — the same path IUCN data takes. If a human-reviewed assessment disagrees with an incoming IUCN assessment, the conflict is flagged for review rather than silently overwritten. One source of truth, auditable back to the assessment record.

geography.title: How coordinates are handled
geography.para1: Occurrence records for non-sensitive species are shown with their original coordinates. For species assessed as threatened (Critically Endangered, Endangered, or Vulnerable), coordinates are generalized to roughly 0.1 degrees — about 11 km at the equator — following GBIF's sensitive-species best practices. This obscures exact localities while preserving the overall distribution pattern, which is what the public map is for.
geography.para2: Exact coordinates for sensitive species are available to conservation coordinator accounts (Tier 3) and above, for the organizations coordinating field work and transfers. The rationale is straightforward: small, range-restricted populations have been targeted by collectors in the past, and obscuring the precise pin is a cheap and effective protection.

caveats.title: Known limitations
caveats.lead: The upstream record is imperfect, and the platform does not pretend otherwise.
caveats.transcriptionBody: A small number of GBIF occurrences place Madagascar endemics in the Indian Ocean, on the wrong coast, or in other countries entirely. These are almost always data-entry or georeferencing errors in the source. The platform flags suspect records for admin review rather than silently dropping them; corrected records are published back where possible.
caveats.undescribedBody1: Madagascar's freshwater fish fauna includes morphospecies awaiting formal description. These appear in the registry under provisional names (for example,
caveats.undescribedBody2: ) with a status of "not yet assessed." They are real species that keepers and researchers are working on; leaving them out would misrepresent the fauna.
caveats.introducedBody: Introduced and invasive species in Malagasy waters (tilapia, gambusia, and others) are hidden from the directory by default to keep the focus on endemics. They can be surfaced with a filter toggle, because they are part of the ecological picture even when they are not the subject of conservation effort.
caveats.husbandryBody: For many species, published keeping and breeding guidance is thin or absent. Profiles say so plainly rather than inventing content.

access.title: What is not public
access.lead: Some data is deliberately behind tier-gated accounts:
access.item2: Per-institution captive inventory detail from ZIMS and partner records (public view shows aggregates only).
access.item3: Breeding-program genetics, studbook-level data, and transfer recommendations.
access.item4: Contact information for individual keepers and researchers.
access.trailing: Access is granted by the platform operator on request to qualified individuals at partner organizations.

interop.title: Standards alignment
interop.body: Occurrence data is modeled against the Darwin Core standard, which is the biodiversity community's shared vocabulary for species records. Publication of the platform's datasets to GBIF via an Integrated Publishing Toolkit (IPT) instance is planned after the MVP. IUCN categories, assessment criteria, and CARES priority status are carried through without re-interpretation.

feedback.title: Questions or corrections
feedback.body: If you see a record that looks wrong, or you have data that should be here and is not, the <aboutLink>About page</aboutLink> has contact and repository links. Corrections are welcomed and credited.

glossary.metaTitle: Glossary — Madagascar Freshwater Fish Conservation Platform
glossary.metaDescription: Definitions of IUCN categories, CARES, SHOAL, Darwin Core, GBIF, ex-situ / in-situ, studbook, and endemic as used on this platform.
glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.intro: Terms used across the platform, defined for a conservation-professional audience. IUCN Red List categories are listed individually; the full category hierarchy and criteria are documented by the IUCN.

cares.definition: The Conservation, Awareness, Recognition, and Encouragement for Species program — a hobbyist-led priority list for freshwater fishes whose wild populations need ex-situ backup. Used on this platform to flag species that are prioritized by the aquarium-keeping community; distinct from the IUCN Red List assessment.

criticallyEndangered.definition: IUCN Red List category for species facing an extremely high risk of extinction in the wild. On this platform, CR is the most severe assessed category displayed.

dataDeficient.definition: IUCN Red List category for species with inadequate information to make a direct or indirect assessment of extinction risk. Not a category of lower concern — a DD species may well be threatened.

darwinCore.definition: The biodiversity data standard maintained by TDWG and used by GBIF for occurrence records. This platform stores occurrence data in Darwin Core–compliant form and can publish it to GBIF as a Darwin Core Archive.

endangered.definition: IUCN Red List category for species facing a very high risk of extinction in the wild, one tier below Critically Endangered.

endemic.definition: Found only in a given area and nowhere else. For this platform, all species in the registry are endemic to Madagascar — native to the island's freshwater systems and not naturally occurring elsewhere.

exSitu.definition: Conservation activity that takes place outside a species' natural habitat — for example, captive breeding at a zoo, public aquarium, or private hobbyist facility. Contrast with in-situ.

gbif.definition: The Global Biodiversity Information Facility — an international network and data infrastructure that aggregates species occurrence records. This platform can publish locality data to GBIF via Darwin Core Archives.

inSitu.definition: Conservation activity that takes place within a species' natural habitat — field surveys, habitat restoration, community-managed protected areas. Contrast with ex-situ.

leastConcern.definition: IUCN Red List category for species that have been evaluated and do not qualify for a threatened category. Among Madagascar's endemic freshwater fish, very few species reach LC.

nearThreatened.definition: IUCN Red List category for species close to qualifying for, or likely to qualify for, a threatened category in the near future.

notEvaluated.definition: A species that has not been evaluated against the IUCN Red List criteria. Public profiles on this platform display "not yet assessed" rather than a category abbreviation; the NE code appears only in space-constrained contexts such as badges and map legends.

shoal.definition: A global conservation initiative focused on freshwater species. Its 1,000 Fishes Blueprint identifies priority species for coordinated action; species flagged as SHOAL priorities are marked on their profile here.

studbook.definition: A structured record of pedigree, demographics, and transfer history for a captive population of a single species. Used to manage genetic diversity and demographic viability across institutions. Studbook-level data on this platform is restricted to program-manager accounts.

threatened.definition: Shorthand for the combined set of Critically Endangered, Endangered, and Vulnerable IUCN categories. Does not include Near Threatened.

vulnerable.definition: IUCN Red List category for species facing a high risk of extinction in the wild, one tier below Endangered.

zims.definition: The Zoological Information Management System, operated by Species360. It is the standard record system for captive animal populations at accredited zoos and aquariums. This platform references ZIMS records where partner institutions have shared access.

coordinator.metaDescription: Ex-situ coordinator triage view: coverage gaps, studbook status, demographic risk, and census staleness.
coordinator.title: Coordinator Dashboard
coordinator.description: A triage view for ex-situ coordinators: where coverage is missing, which captive populations are at demographic risk, and whose census is overdue. The registry is the source of truth — this page is a read-only summary computed from it.
coordinator.tierNote: Population-level detail. Visible to Tier 3+ accounts (Conservation Coordinator and above).

configError.title: Coordinator data is temporarily unavailable.
configError.body: The panels below couldn't reach the coordination API. Try refreshing the page; if the issue persists, contact the platform team.

coverage.title: Coverage gap
coverage.captionShort: Threatened species with no ex-situ backstop.
coverage.captionFull: Critically Endangered, Endangered, and Vulnerable species with no ex-situ population on record. The first list a coordinator should triage: each row is a species that has no captive safety net at all.
coverage.unavailable: Coverage data is temporarily unavailable. The view will populate once the coordination API is reachable again.
coverage.noResults: No threatened species are missing ex-situ coverage at this filter. Either every threatened species in scope has a captive population on record, or the filter has narrowed the list to zero.
coverage.ddBody: {total} species ({endemic} endemic) need a current Red List assessment before their conservation status can be acted on.
coverage.ddNone: No DD species in the registry.

studbook.title: Studbook status
studbook.captionFull: Where each species sits on the captive-management spectrum, from formally managed studbooks down to no holdings at all. The breeding-not-studbook row matters: it surfaces ad-hoc populations that are reproducing but whose pedigree isn't tracked — a fragility that gets hidden when those species are lumped in with studbook programs.
studbook.unavailable: Studbook status is temporarily unavailable. The view will populate once the coordination API is reachable again.

studbook_managed.label: Studbook-managed
studbook_managed.hint: Coordinated program with pedigree records and transfer plans.

breeding_not_studbook.label: Breeding, not studbook
breeding_not_studbook.hint: Breeding observed, but no formal studbook — pedigree is not tracked.

holdings_only.label: Holdings only
holdings_only.hint: Held in captivity, no breeding recorded.

sexRatio.title: Sex-ratio risk
sexRatio.captionFull: Captive populations whose sex composition limits breeding potential. M.F.U is the standard zoo notation: males.females.unsexed. A population is flagged when one sex is absent, when the male-to-female ratio exceeds 4:1, or when more than half the animals are unsexed.
sexRatio.unavailable: Demographic data is temporarily unavailable. Once the coordination API is reachable again, populations with imbalance will appear here.
sexRatio.noResults: No populations are currently flagged for sex-ratio risk.

staleCensus.title: Stale census
staleCensus.captionShort: Populations overdue for a census update.
staleCensus.captionFull: Populations whose last census record — or any holding update — is more than {threshold} months old, plus any population that has never been censused. A current count is the prerequisite for every other coordination decision.
staleCensus.unavailable: Census timestamps are temporarily unavailable. Overdue populations will reappear once the coordination API is reachable.
staleCensus.noResults: Every tracked population has been censused within the last {threshold} months.

transfers.title: Transfer activity
transfers.captionFull: Proposed, approved, and in-transit moves between institutions, plus moves completed in the last {windowDays} days. In-flight transfers are sorted oldest-proposed first, so anything stuck in coordination floats to the top.
transfers.unavailable: Transfer records are temporarily unavailable. Proposed, in-transit, and recently completed moves will reappear once the coordination API is reachable.
transfers.inFlightEmpty: No transfers are currently proposed, approved, or in transit.
transfers.recentEmpty: No transfers have been completed in the last {windowDays} days.

openRecs.title: Open breeding recommendations
openRecs.captionFull: Active breed, hold, and transfer items issued by coordinators, sorted by priority. Critical and high-priority rows surface first; completed and cancelled items are archived elsewhere.
openRecs.unavailable: Open recommendations are temporarily unavailable. Breed, hold, and transfer items will reappear once the coordination API is reachable.
openRecs.noResults: No open recommendations on file. Either the breeding plan is fully addressed or coordinators have not yet logged any items for the current period.

reproductive.title: Recent reproductive activity
reproductive.titleWithCount: Recent reproductive activity — {count, plural, one {# event} other {# events}}
reproductive.captionFull: Population-level events recorded in the last {windowDays} days, newest first: spawning, hatching, mortality, acquisition, and disposition. Up to {limit} rows are listed here; a population's full event history is on its admin page.
reproductive.unavailable: Breeding event records are temporarily unavailable. Recent spawnings, hatchings, and other population-level events will reappear once the coordination API is reachable.
reproductive.noResults: No events have been logged in the last {windowDays} days. Coordinators can record spawning, hatching, mortality, and acquisition events from a population's admin page; an empty ledger here usually means events are happening but are not being logged in the registry.

account.metaDescription: Your account profile and access tier.
account.title: Your profile

tierDescriptions.1: Public profiles, the distribution map, and the conservation dashboard.
tierDescriptions.2: Adds occurrence datasets, published field-program summaries, and observation submission.
tierDescriptions.3: Adds exact locality coordinates for sensitive species, breeding recommendations, and transfer coordination.
tierDescriptions.4: Adds population genetics, studbook-level data, and per-institution captive inventory.
tierDescriptions.5: Full system access, including user management and data import.

account.tierUpgradeHint: Higher tiers are assigned by the platform operator on request, for staff at partner organizations.

institutionMembership.heading: Institution membership
institutionMembership.approved: Your membership for {name} is approved. You can edit your institution's populations directly.
institutionMembership.pending: Your claim for {name} is pending coordinator review. We'll email you when it's approved.
institutionMembership.rejected: Your claim for {name} was not approved. If this is in error, reply to the rejection email or contact the platform team.
institutionMembership.withdrawn: Your claim for {name} was withdrawn. You can submit a fresh claim by signing up again or contacting the platform team.

account.transientError: Your profile is temporarily unavailable. Refresh in a moment; your sign-in is still active. If the problem persists, the platform team's contact details are on the About page.

localePicker.label: Preferred language
localePicker.help: Used for transactional emails and the default UI language on first visit. Changes apply immediately.
localePicker.error: Couldn't save. Try again in a moment.

husbandry.metaTitle: Contribute husbandry updates — Madagascar Freshwater Fish
husbandry.title: Contribute husbandry updates
husbandry.bodyPrefix: The contribution form is not yet live. In the meantime, please email
husbandry.bodySuffix: with your corrections or additional content. Reference the species URL so we can route the update to the right record.

disclaimer.para1: This guidance reflects practices reported by keepers and drawn from published sources. It is not a protocol, and conditions vary between systems, regions, and individual fish.
disclaimer.para2: Use it as a starting point, compare it against other references, and consult a qualified aquatic veterinarian for health concerns.

husbandry.showMoreFactors: {count, plural, one {Show # more factor} other {Show # more factors}}
husbandry.metaTitleNotFound: Husbandry not found — Madagascar Freshwater Fish

errorState.title: Husbandry guidance temporarily unavailable
errorState.body: The husbandry data service is unreachable. Try again in a moment.

review.stale: Review pending — content may be out of date.

related.heldAt: {count, plural, one {Held at # institution.} other {Held at # institutions.}}

login.metaDescription: Sign in to access tier-restricted content.
login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Sign in unlocks tier-restricted views — exact locality coordinates for sensitive species, the coordinator dashboard, and partner-only data. Species profiles, the public map, and the conservation dashboard are open without an account.
login.verifiedBanner: Email verified. Sign in to continue.
login.errorRateLimited: Too many attempts. Please wait a few minutes and try again.

signup.metaTitle: Create an account — Madagascar Freshwater Fish
signup.metaDescription: Create a researcher account to access tier-restricted occurrence data and submit observations.
signup.title: Create an account
signup.subtitle: New accounts start as Tier 2 (Registered Researcher), which adds access to occurrence datasets and observation submission. Higher tiers — for ex-situ coordinators and program managers — are assigned by the platform operator on request. Public profiles, the map, and the conservation dashboard remain open without an account.
signup.passwordHelp: At least 12 characters. Avoid common passwords and anything close to your email or name.
signup.submittedTitle: Check your email to finish signing up.
signup.submittedBody: If an account can be created with that address, a verification link is on its way. The link is valid for 48 hours. If nothing arrives, check your spam folder, then try registering again.

actionErrors.missingFields: Email, name, and password are all required.
actionErrors.unreachable: Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
actionErrors.genericFailure: Sign-up failed. Please check your details and try again.
actionErrors.unavailable: The server is unavailable. Please try again in a moment.

verify.metaTitle: Verify your account — Madagascar Freshwater Fish
verify.metaDescription: Activate your account by verifying the email link.
verify.title: Verification link

missingToken.title: This link is missing its verification token.
missingToken.bodyPrefix: Open the link directly from your verification email — the URL should end with
missingToken.bodyMiddle: . If your email client truncated the link, you can

expired.title: This verification link is no longer valid.
expired.bodyPrefix: Links expire 48 hours after sign-up, and each link can only be used once. To finish creating your account,
expired.bodySuffix: and we'll send a new link to your email.

transient.title: We couldn't verify your account just now.
transient.bodyPrefix: The verification service is temporarily unreachable. Try the link again in a few minutes. If it keeps failing, the platform team can verify your account manually — contact details are on the

errors.title: This page could not be loaded
errors.subtitle: An unexpected error interrupted rendering. Try again in a moment, or return to the home page.

notFound.metaTitle: Page not found — Madagascar Freshwater Fish
notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: This address does not match any page on the platform. The link may be out of date, or the page may have moved.

footer.license: Madagascar Freshwater Fish Conservation Platform — Apache-2.0
footer.dataNote: Data mirrored from IUCN, FishBase, GBIF, ZIMS, SHOAL, and CARES.
